skill.name: stt
skill.description: Transcribe audio to text using OpenAI Whisper through the verging.ai proxy API.
skill.summary: Transcribe audio to text using OpenAI Whisper through the verging.ai proxy API.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review shows these are Markdown code fences, shell examples, and table text rather than executable skill code. The confirmed risk is expected network use: users upload audio files and send an API key to the verging.ai STT endpoint, so publication should include a privacy and credential warning.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Audio Upload and API Key Transmission
medium_findings.description: The documented workflow sends audio files to https://verging.ai/api/v1/ai/stt with an Authorization header containing a verging.ai API key. This is expected for the skill, but users may upload sensitive speech and should understand the third-party processing and credential exposure model.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint, file upload form field, and Authorization header are explicitly documented. The behavior is legitimate STT functionality, but it creates a real privacy and credential-handling risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are caused by Markdown fenced examples for installation, environment setup, curl usage, sample response, and error response text. No executable Ruby backtick code or hidden script file is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched backticks are Markdown delimiters around documentation examples. There is no evidence of Ruby code execution in README.md or SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to implemented cryptographic functions. The reviewed lines contain skill metadata, parameter documentation, and error table content, with no hashing, encryption, or signature logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited sections are prose and Markdown tables. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner combined code execution, network, and credential-access signals, but review shows no executable code path. The remaining confirmed behavior is an explicit documented API call to the service the skill is designed to use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network and API key use are real, but the code execution premise is not supported by the reviewed files. No prompt injection or obfuscated behavior was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated File Upload to External API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents a curl command that uploads a local audio file to the verging.ai STT endpoint using an API key in the Authorization header. This pattern should be treated as sensitive because audio and credentials leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command directly includes the remote endpoint, Authorization header, and multipart file upload. The pattern is intentional and documented, not hidden.

content.value_statement: Audio files are hard to review, search, and reuse without accurate text. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code send supported audio files to verging.ai for Whisper-based transcription.
actual_capabilities: Documents how to authenticate with a verging.ai API key.
Shows how to send multipart audio uploads to the STT endpoint.
Supports MP3, MP4, MPEG, MPGA, M4A, WAV, and WebM audio formats.
Allows optional language, response format, model, and temperature parameters.
Documents response fields, credit usage, and common error codes.
limitations: Requires a verging.ai account and API key.
Uploads audio to a third-party service for processing.
Limits individual audio files to 25 MB.
Accuracy depends on the source audio quality and language clarity.

use_cases.title: Convert Meeting Recordings
use_cases.description: Transcribe recorded meetings so teams can summarize decisions and search discussion history.

use_cases.title: Process Voice Notes
use_cases.description: Turn short spoken notes into text that can be edited, organized, or shared.

use_cases.title: Prepare Research Interviews
use_cases.description: Create transcripts from interview audio before coding themes or extracting quotes.

prompt_templates.title: Transcribe One File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the stt skill to transcribe recording.mp3 in English. Tell me the resulting transcript and any service errors.

prompt_templates.title: Transcribe With Language
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the stt skill to transcribe interview.m4a with language set to en. Return a clean transcript for review.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Format Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the stt skill documentation to explain which response format I should request for a transcript I want to summarize later.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan transcription for several audio files under the 25 MB limit, including language settings, privacy checks, and credit expectations.

output_examples.input: A user asks to transcribe a short English meeting recording.
output_examples.output: A plain-text transcript of the meeting, plus a note that one credit is consumed per started minute.

output_examples.input: A user provides an unsupported or oversized audio file.
output_examples.output: A concise error explanation that the file must use a supported audio format and stay within the 25 MB limit.

output_examples.input: A user asks how to authenticate requests.
output_examples.output: Step-by-step guidance to create a verging.ai API key and send it in the Authorization header.

best_practices: Review audio for sensitive personal or business information before uploading it.
Store the API key in an environment variable instead of writing it into prompts or files.
Specify the language when it is known to improve transcription reliability.
anti_patterns: Do not upload confidential recordings without approval from the data owner.
Do not paste real API keys into shared chats, tickets, or documentation.
Do not split files in a way that loses context needed for accurate transcripts.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It documents how to transcribe audio files through the verging.ai speech-to-text API.

faq.question: Which audio formats are supported?
faq.answer: The documentation lists MP3, MP4, MPEG, MPGA, M4A, WAV, and WebM.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. Requests require a verging.ai API key sent in the Authorization header.

faq.question: Does audio stay local?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow uploads audio to verging.ai for transcription.

faq.question: What is the file size limit?
faq.answer: The documented maximum file size is 25 MB per audio file.

faq.question: Can it detect the language automatically?
faq.answer: The README lists automatic language detection, and the API also accepts an optional language parameter.
